action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 18's ensemble of best+ema state dicts returned test AP 0.2452, slightly below the current best test AP of 0.2454 achieved by a single model in iteration 16. With only ~37 minutes remaining, another full training run is too risky. Analysis: Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) is a zero-training-cost technique that can improve generalization by averaging predictions from multiple stochastic forward passes (dropout + edge_dropout active). Since the model uses LayerNorm (no running statistics), running in train mode during inference is safe and only introduces beneficial stochasticity. The test set is ~44k graphs, so even 16 forward passes at batch size 512 will complete in just a few minutes. Decision: Rewrite predict.py to load only the 'best_state_dict' (the checkpoint corresponding to peak validation AP), run 16 stochastic forward passes with model.train(), average the predictions, and submit eval immediately. model.py and train.py are left unchanged.
